title: Chronofest
short_description: Public Talk with Marina Davydova and Artur Ghukasyan

narratives.full_description: In this public conversation, renowned theatre critic, playwright, director, and curator Marina Davydova, and actor, producer, and director of the HighFest International Performing Arts Festival Artur Ghukasyan will explore the powerful influence of festivals on contemporary theatre.
narratives.target_audience_description:
How does the international festival circuit reshape theatre as we know it? In this public conversation, renowned theatre critic, playwright, director, and curator Marina Davydova, and actor, producer, and director of the HighFest International Performing Arts Festival Artur Ghukasyan will explore the powerful influence of festivals on contemporary theatre. 
 
 They will discuss what makes festivals in Europe essential today, which emerging voices are worth watching, and how international festivals shape not only the artistic landscape but also the cultural dynamics of the countries where they take place.

A Public Discussion on the Role of Performing Arts Today 
 
 In the discussion “What’s the Point of It All?”, participants from various countries and cultural backgrounds will share how they currently understand the purpose and role of the performing arts. Does it serve a social mission? If so, what does that entail? How does the landscape of independent art shift depending on political, economic, and cultural contexts? 
 
 We’ll explore the tension between an artist’s inner drive and external demands — from audiences and institutions. We’ll talk about disappointment and hope, about responsibility, meaning, and the absence of it.

• Pooya Jafari, assistant director of the Body Pain theatre (Iran) 
 • Anahit Ghazaryan, writer and dramaturge 
 • Alexander Plotnikov, theatre director, writer 
 • Ilya Moshchitsky – co-curator of Chronofest, theater director. 
 • Gritt Uldall-Jessen, independent playwright and AD of Det Frie Felts Festival/ Selected Works — a festival dedicated to the independent field of performing arts in Denmark. 
 Moderator : Andriesh Gandrabur - Programme and Vision Lead at hosq
